title: kage sync rebase needs the kage-sync identity fallback (CI had no git identity)
summary: git rebase/ continue/ skip recreate commits and need committer identity; CI runners have none, so the two kage sync tests failed only on CI while passing on macOS Apple git auto derives an identity in paths where the run
body: git rebase/--continue/--skip recreate commits and need committer identity; CI runners have none, so the two kage sync tests failed only on CI while passing on macOS (Apple git auto-derives an identity in paths where the runner's git refuses). Fix: rebaseOntoUpstream prepends syncIdentityArgs (-c user.name=kage-sync -c user.email=kage-sync@localhost) to every rebase invocation, same as commits. Debugging convention adopted: sync test asserts embed JSON.stringify(result.errors) in the assertion message so CI failures name the exact git error instead of false!==true.
